title: monarda punctata subsp. stanfieldii
intro: Monarda punctata subsp. stanfieldii, also known as the bearded horsemint, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that have been used to treat a range of health issues. The bearded horsemint has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, coughs, and sore throats, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting relaxation. Herbal preparations of bearded horsemint can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
